speaker: CASSIUS
text: That you have wronged me doth appear in this:

speaker: CASSIUS
text: You have condemned and noted Lucius Pella

speaker: CASSIUS
text: For taking bribes here of the Sardians,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Wherein my letters, praying on his side

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Because I knew the man, was slighted off.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: You wronged yourself to write in such a case.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: In such a time as this it is not meet

speaker: CASSIUS
text: That every nice offense should bear his comment.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let me tell you, Cassius, you yourself

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Are much condemned to have an itching palm,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To sell and mart your offices for gold

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To undeservers.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I an itching palm?

speaker: CASSIUS
text: You know that you are Brutus that speaks this,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Or, by the gods, this speech were else your last.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The name of Cassius honors this corruption,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And chastisement doth therefore hide his head.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Chastisement?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Remember March; the ides of March remember.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Did not great Julius bleed for justice’ sake?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: What villain touched his body that did stab

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And not for justice? What, shall one of us

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That struck the foremost man of all this world

speaker: BRUTUS
text: But for supporting robbers, shall we now

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Contaminate our fingers with base bribes

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And sell the mighty space of our large honors

speaker: BRUTUS
text: For so much trash as may be graspèd thus?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I had rather be a dog and bay the moon

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Than such a Roman.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Brutus, bait not me.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I’ll not endure it. You forget yourself

speaker: CASSIUS
text: To hedge me in. I am a soldier, I,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Older in practice, abler than yourself

speaker: CASSIUS
text: To make conditions.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Go to! You are not, Cassius.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I am.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I say you are not.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Urge me no more. I shall forget myself.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Have mind upon your health. Tempt me no farther.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Away, slight man!

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Is ’t possible?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Hear me, for I will speak.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Must I give way and room to your rash choler?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Shall I be frighted when a madman stares?

speaker: CASSIUS
text: O you gods, you gods, must I endure all this?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: All this? Ay, more. Fret till your proud heart break.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Go show your slaves how choleric you are

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And make your bondmen tremble. Must I budge?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Must I observe you? Must I stand and crouch

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Under your testy humor? By the gods,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: You shall digest the venom of your spleen

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Though it do split you. For, from this day forth,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I’ll use you for my mirth, yea, for my laughter,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: When you are waspish.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Is it come to this?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: You say you are a better soldier.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let it appear so, make your vaunting true,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And it shall please me well. For mine own part,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I shall be glad to learn of noble men.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: You wrong me every way, you wrong me, Brutus.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I said an elder soldier, not a better.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Did I say “better”?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: If you did, I care not.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: When Caesar lived he durst not thus have moved me.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Peace, peace! You durst not so have tempted him.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I durst not?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: No.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: What? Durst not tempt him?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: For your life you durst not.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Do not presume too much upon my love.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I may do that I shall be sorry for.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: You have done that you should be sorry for.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: There is no terror, Cassius, in your threats,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: For I am armed so strong in honesty

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That they pass by me as the idle wind,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Which I respect not. I did send to you

speaker: BRUTUS
text: For certain sums of gold, which you denied me,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: For I can raise no money by vile means.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: By heaven, I had rather coin my heart

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And drop my blood for drachmas than to wring

speaker: BRUTUS
text: From the hard hands of peasants their vile trash

speaker: BRUTUS
text: By any indirection. I did send

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To you for gold to pay my legions,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Which you denied me. Was that done like Cassius?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Should I have answered Caius Cassius so?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: When Marcus Brutus grows so covetous

speaker: BRUTUS
text: To lock such rascal counters from his friends,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Be ready, gods, with all your thunderbolts;

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Dash him to pieces!

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I denied you not.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: You did.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I did not. He was but a fool that brought

speaker: CASSIUS
text: My answer back. Brutus hath rived my heart.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: A friend should bear his friend’s infirmities,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: But Brutus makes mine greater than they are.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I do not, till you practice them on me.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: You love me not.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I do not like your faults.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: A friendly eye could never see such faults.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: A flatterer’s would not, though they do appear

speaker: BRUTUS
text: As huge as high Olympus.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Come, Antony, and young Octavius, come!

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Revenge yourselves alone on Cassius,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: For Cassius is aweary of the world—

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Hated by one he loves, braved by his brother,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Checked like a bondman, all his faults observed,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Set in a notebook, learned and conned by rote

speaker: CASSIUS
text: To cast into my teeth. O, I could weep

speaker: CASSIUS
text: My spirit from mine eyes! There is my dagger,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And here my naked breast; within, a heart

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Dearer than Pluto’s mine, richer than gold.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: If that thou be’st a Roman, take it forth.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I that denied thee gold will give my heart.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Strike as thou didst at Caesar, for I know

speaker: CASSIUS
text: When thou didst hate him worst, thou lovedst him better

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Than ever thou lovedst Cassius.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Sheathe your dagger.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Be angry when you will, it shall have scope.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Do what you will, dishonor shall be humor.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: O Cassius, you are yokèd with a lamb

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That carries anger as the flint bears fire,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Who, much enforcèd, shows a hasty spark

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And straight is cold again.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Hath Cassius lived

speaker: CASSIUS
text: To be but mirth and laughter to his Brutus

speaker: CASSIUS
text: When grief and blood ill-tempered vexeth him?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: When I spoke that, I was ill-tempered too.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Do you confess so much? Give me your hand.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And my heart too.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: O Brutus!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: What’s the matter?

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Have not you love enough to bear with me

speaker: CASSIUS
text: When that rash humor which my mother gave me

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Makes me forgetful?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Yes, Cassius, and from henceforth

speaker: BRUTUS
text: When you are over-earnest with your Brutus,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: He’ll think your mother chides, and leave you so.

speaker: POET
text: Let me go in to see the Generals.

speaker: POET
text: There is some grudge between ’em; ’tis not meet

speaker: POET
text: They be alone.

speaker: LUCILIUS
text: You shall not come to them.

speaker: POET
text: Nothing but death shall stay me.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: How now, what’s the matter?

speaker: POET
text: For shame, you generals, what do you mean?

speaker: POET
text: Love and be friends as two such men should be,

speaker: POET
text: For I have seen more years, I’m sure, than ye.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Ha, ha, how vilely doth this cynic rhyme!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Get you hence, sirrah! Saucy fellow, hence!

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Bear with him, Brutus. ’Tis his fashion.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I’ll know his humor when he knows his time.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: What should the wars do with these jigging fools?—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Companion, hence!

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Away, away, be gone!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Lucilius and Titinius, bid the commanders

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Prepare to lodge their companies tonight.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And come yourselves, and bring Messala with you

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Immediately to us.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Lucius, a bowl of wine.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I did not think you could have been so angry.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: O Cassius, I am sick of many griefs.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Of your philosophy you make no use

speaker: CASSIUS
text: If you give place to accidental evils.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: No man bears sorrow better. Portia is dead.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Ha? Portia?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: She is dead.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: How ’scaped I killing when I crossed you so?

speaker: CASSIUS
text: O insupportable and touching loss!

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Upon what sickness?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Impatient of my absence,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And grief that young Octavius with Mark Antony

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Have made themselves so strong—for with her death

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That tidings came—with this she fell distract

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And, her attendants absent, swallowed fire.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: And died so?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Even so.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: O you immortal gods!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Speak no more of her.—Give me a bowl of wine.—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: In this I bury all unkindness, Cassius.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: My heart is thirsty for that noble pledge.—

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Fill, Lucius, till the wine o’erswell the cup;

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I cannot drink too much of Brutus’ love.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Come in, Titinius. Welcome, good Messala.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Now sit we close about this taper here,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And call in question our necessities.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Portia, art thou gone?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: No more, I pray you.—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Messala, I have here receivèd letters

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That young Octavius and Mark Antony

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Come down upon us with a mighty power,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Bending their expedition toward Philippi.

speaker: MESSALA
text: Myself have letters of the selfsame tenor.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: With what addition?

speaker: MESSALA
text: That by proscription and bills of outlawry,

speaker: MESSALA
text: Octavius, Antony, and Lepidus

speaker: MESSALA
text: Have put to death an hundred senators.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Therein our letters do not well agree.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Mine speak of seventy senators that died

speaker: BRUTUS
text: By their proscriptions, Cicero being one.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Cicero one?

speaker: MESSALA
text: Cicero is dead,

speaker: MESSALA
text: And by that order of proscription.

speaker: MESSALA
text: Had you your letters from your wife, my lord?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: No, Messala.

speaker: MESSALA
text: Nor nothing in your letters writ of her?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Nothing, Messala.

speaker: MESSALA
text: That methinks is strange.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Why ask you? Hear you aught of her in yours?

speaker: MESSALA
text: No, my lord.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Now, as you are a Roman, tell me true.

speaker: MESSALA
text: Then like a Roman bear the truth I tell,

speaker: MESSALA
text: For certain she is dead, and by strange manner.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Why, farewell, Portia. We must die, Messala.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: With meditating that she must die once,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I have the patience to endure it now.

speaker: MESSALA
text: Even so great men great losses should endure.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I have as much of this in art as you,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: But yet my nature could not bear it so.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Well, to our work alive. What do you think

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Of marching to Philippi presently?

speaker: CASSIUS
text: I do not think it good.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Your reason?

speaker: CASSIUS
text: This it is:

speaker: CASSIUS
text: ’Tis better that the enemy seek us;

speaker: CASSIUS
text: So shall he waste his means, weary his soldiers,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Doing himself offense, whilst we, lying still,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Are full of rest, defense, and nimbleness.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Good reasons must of force give place to better.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The people ’twixt Philippi and this ground

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Do stand but in a forced affection,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: For they have grudged us contribution.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The enemy, marching along by them,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: By them shall make a fuller number up,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Come on refreshed, new-added, and encouraged,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: From which advantage shall we cut him off

speaker: BRUTUS
text: If at Philippi we do face him there,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: These people at our back.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Hear me, good brother—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Under your pardon. You must note besides

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That we have tried the utmost of our friends,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Our legions are brim full, our cause is ripe.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The enemy increaseth every day;

speaker: BRUTUS
text: We, at the height, are ready to decline.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: There is a tide in the affairs of men

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Which, taken at the flood, leads on to fortune;

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Omitted, all the voyage of their life

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Is bound in shallows and in miseries.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: On such a full sea are we now afloat,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And we must take the current when it serves

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Or lose our ventures.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Then, with your will, go on;

speaker: CASSIUS
text: We’ll along ourselves and meet them at Philippi.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: The deep of night is crept upon our talk,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And nature must obey necessity,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Which we will niggard with a little rest.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: There is no more to say.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: No more. Good night.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Early tomorrow will we rise and hence.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Lucius.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: My gown.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Farewell, good Messala.—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Good night, Titinius.—Noble, noble Cassius,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Good night and good repose.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: O my dear brother,

speaker: CASSIUS
text: This was an ill beginning of the night.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Never come such division ’tween our souls!

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Let it not, Brutus.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Everything is well.

speaker: CASSIUS
text: Good night, my lord.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Good night, good brother.

speaker:
TITINIUS
              /
              MESSALA
text: Good night, Lord Brutus.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Farewell, everyone.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Give me the gown. Where is thy instrument?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Here in the tent.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: What, thou speak’st drowsily?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Poor knave, I blame thee not; thou art o’erwatched.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Call Claudius and some other of my men;

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I’ll have them sleep on cushions in my tent.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Varro and Claudius.

speaker: VARRO
text: Calls my lord?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I pray you, sirs, lie in my tent and sleep.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It may be I shall raise you by and by

speaker: BRUTUS
text: On business to my brother Cassius.

speaker: VARRO
text: So please you, we will stand and watch your pleasure.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I will not have it so. Lie down, good sirs.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It may be I shall otherwise bethink me.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Look, Lucius, here’s the book I sought for so.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I put it in the pocket of my gown.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I was sure your Lordship did not give it me.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Bear with me, good boy, I am much forgetful.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Canst thou hold up thy heavy eyes awhile

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And touch thy instrument a strain or two?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Ay, my lord, an ’t please you.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It does, my boy.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I trouble thee too much, but thou art willing.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: It is my duty, sir.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I should not urge thy duty past thy might.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I know young bloods look for a time of rest.

speaker: LUCIUS
text: I have slept, my lord, already.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It was well done, and thou shalt sleep again.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I will not hold thee long. If I do live,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I will be good to thee.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: This is a sleepy tune. O murd’rous slumber,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Layest thou thy leaden mace upon my boy,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That plays thee music?—Gentle knave, good night.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I will not do thee so much wrong to wake thee.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: If thou dost nod, thou break’st thy instrument.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I’ll take it from thee and, good boy, good night.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Let me see, let me see; is not the leaf turned down

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Where I left reading? Here it is, I think.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: How ill this taper burns.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Ha, who comes here?—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: I think it is the weakness of mine eyes

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That shapes this monstrous apparition.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: It comes upon me.—Art thou any thing?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Art thou some god, some angel, or some devil,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: That mak’st my blood cold and my hair to stare?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Speak to me what thou art.

speaker: GHOST
text: Thy evil spirit, Brutus.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Why com’st thou?

speaker: GHOST
text: To tell thee thou shalt see me at Philippi.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Well, then I shall see thee again?

speaker: GHOST
text: Ay, at Philippi.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Why, I will see thee at Philippi, then.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Now I have taken heart, thou vanishest.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Ill spirit, I would hold more talk with thee.—

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Boy, Lucius!—Varro, Claudius, sirs, awake!

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Claudius!

speaker: LUCIUS
text: The strings, my lord, are false.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: He thinks he still is at his instrument.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Lucius, awake!

speaker: LUCIUS
text: My lord?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Didst thou dream, Lucius, that thou so criedst out?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: My lord, I do not know that I did cry.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Yes, that thou didst. Didst thou see anything?

speaker: LUCIUS
text: Nothing, my lord.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Sleep again, Lucius.—Sirrah Claudius!

speaker: BRUTUS
text:
To
                 
                Varro
                .
                 
              Fellow thou, awake!

speaker: VARRO
text: My lord?

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: My lord?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Why did you so cry out, sirs, in your sleep?

speaker: BOTH
text: Did we, my lord?

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Ay. Saw you anything?

speaker: VARRO
text: No, my lord, I saw nothing.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: Nor I, my lord.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Go and commend me to my brother Cassius.

speaker: BRUTUS
text: Bid him set on his powers betimes before,

speaker: BRUTUS
text: And we will follow.

speaker: BOTH
text: It shall be done, my lord.
